mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS levels (Rank IC < 0.02) across the full sample period.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns (interaction term coefficient insignificant at 5% level in out-of-sample regression).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity utilization, which translates to higher corporate profit margins and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases yield negative 20-day forward returns in > 60% of observations during early-cycle expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls indicate strengthening economic activity, boosting investor confidence and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero predictive power for 5-day forward returns (Rank IC consistently near zero across rolling 3-year windows).

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index growth exceeding 2.5% year-over-year triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 2.5% YoY are followed by positive 60-day returns in > 50% of cases, negating the tightening channel.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly retail sales validates sustained aggregate demand, reinforcing earnings expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction effect between GDPC1 and RSAFS growth rates becomes statistically insignificant in predicting 20-day returns during mid-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive months of declining industrial production signal a manufacturing contraction, prompting immediate portfolio de-risking and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Consecutive INDPRO declines are followed by positive 5-day returns in > 55% of observations during late-cycle contraction phases.
